stem: The equation h = (9/5)(v - 273.15) + 32 gives the corresponding temperature h, in degrees Fahrenheit, of any substance that has a temperature of v kelvins, where v > 0. If a substance has a temperature of 134 degrees Fahrenheit, what is the corresponding temperature, in kelvins, of this substance?
rationale: Substituting 134 for h: 134 = (9/5)(v - 273.15) + 32. Subtract 32: 102 = (9/5)(v - 273.15). Multiply both sides by 5/9: 56.67 ≈ v - 273.15. Adding 273.15: v ≈ 329.82 kelvins.
